# Chapter 2: Demand Scenarios

## Synopsis

Chapter 2 breaks down final energy demand by sector (industry, buildings,
transport, agriculture) and region under each scenario through 2050. It
identifies electrification rates and efficiency gains as the two primary demand
levers distinguishing the pathways.

## Portfolio Lens

Through the energy-transition lens, electrification of end-use sectors is the
single largest driver of emissions reduction on the demand side. Buildings and
light-duty transport approach near-full electrification under the net-zero path
by 2040, while heavy industry lags due to process-heat constraints.

## Key numbers and projections

- Final energy demand falls 8% by 2030 under net-zero despite GDP growth
- Electricity share of final energy rises from 23% (2023) to 40% (2030) in NZE
- Industrial energy demand remains roughly flat due to efficiency offsetting growth

## Open questions

- Whether behavioral change assumptions in the transport sector are achievable
- Impact of emerging AI data-center load on electricity demand projections
